What Attributes Govern Make a strength roll when a character tries to: * Lift something heavier than he is. * Cary something heavier than he for more than a few feet * Break or bend an object which is dificult to break or bend (eg, a bot, a malfunction- ing cone rifle, et). Haul someone over a walkway. * Be,

Make an endurance roll when a character 
‘Avoid passing out when incapacitated. ‘Run over a long period of time or over great distances, * Go without food, water or rest for an extend- ed period of tin * Avoid deleterious effects of massive doses of pharmaceuticals, * Avoid whimpering when being treated by a docbot * tc 
Make a moxie roll when a character tries t: ‘* Decipher abstruse mathematics without a reference. ‘Notice something at a great distance. ‘© Notice an unusual scent or taste. + Fic 
‘Make a chutzpah roll when a character tries to: ** Walk down a busy higher clearance corridor without being noticed, * Goose a Vulture warrior and make him think someone else did it * Demand a refund for a shoddy item from Puc * Ftc Make a mechanical aptitude roll when ‘character tries to: * Examine and understand a device he's never, seen before. * Figure out how an electronic device works ithout a circuit diagram, * Fix his chute, which failed to open when it, should have, in the final ten seconds before impact Etc, 
Make a power roll when: ‘© A character tries to use his mutant power. * If you need a “luck” roll — that is, none of the character's other skills or attributes seem to have any bearing on the problem at hand. 
este ee Difficulty ‘Whenever a character uses an attribute — cor a skill, for that matter — the gamemaster should decide how easy or difficult the task is ifthe task is particulary dificult, he should reduce the character’ attibute or skill number before the rolls made; if particularly easy, he should increase it Difficulty isn't the only factor you should take into account when modifying a character’ attribute numbers, The charm and relative wit of the players presentation is im- portant, to0. The purpose of the fame is to fave fun, and ifthe player is being amusing, witty and intelligent, he should be rewarded. ‘On the other hand, If he's being dull as dishwater, he must suffer the consequences. Those who contribute t others’ enjoyment of the game tumph; dullads die, Here are some rules of thumbs Player Wit Attribute Difficulty: & Charm: Modification: easy scintillating x2 normal acceptable no change tough Uunremarkable x Ys difficult dull xh 
Attributes and Skill Bases Consider this a character's agility is 15, That ‘means his agility skill base is 4, So if he hasn't spent any skill points on a particular agility based skill — neurowhip, say — his skill 
number is 4. I he uses the skill, he’s only got 4 chances out of 20 of doing so successfully. But if he uses his agility to do something ‘which no skill governs — balance on a high wire, for example — he rolls against his agili- ty. That gives him 15 chances out of 20 of suc ceeding. Therefore, you'd rather not use your skills i you can avoid it, Right? ‘Wrong, When no skill governs a particular action and a character has no taining in how to per- form it and is relying on his innate bodily abilities, his chances should be slim, Unless an attribute is being used for something that is patently pretty easy, you should generally presume that the task is df ficult, meaning the character's skill number is quartered. So our character's ‘agility of 15 would become 4.25, rounded to 4, before his high= wire roll would be made.
